title: The Safe Transport Blueprint for Large Outdoor Water Fountains and Courtyard Statues
excerpt: Outdoor water fountain moving: courtyard statues transport, rigging heavy stone, drain winterize, garden feature relocation blueprint Addis Ababa villas.
content:
The safe transport blueprint for large outdoor water fountains and courtyard statues

Courtyard fountains and statues anchor villa identity—stone mass, plumbing fragile, bases crack if tilted like dresser. Safe transport blueprint covers drain, disassemble tiers, rigging rated for weight, and destination pad level before water flows again.

This guide transports large outdoor fountains and courtyard statues safely.

Pre-move fountain protocol

  • Electric pump off; water drained completely; lines blown or sponged dry.
  • Disconnect plumbing; cap fittings; bag hardware labeled.
  • Clean algae dry—slip hazard and odor truck.
  • Photo plumbing routing reassembly reference.

Statue and stone assessment

Weight estimate; lift points; crack photo pre-existing. Small statues crate; large rigging forklift or crane survey. Vertical orientation strap padded—no face strap on carved detail.

Disassembly strategy

Multi-tier fountains often separate bowls—pad each tier foam nest box. Rebar or pin connectors bag per tier number.

Transport and placement

Low forward truck strapped; plywood between stone and metal contact. Destination pad level concrete before set—tilt cracks bowl. Plumber reconnect pressure test before fill.

Weather

Rain move: cover stone; no wet crate sealed trapping moisture. Winterize not issue Addis but dust seal ports.

Insurance and landscape

Declared value; path protection lawn and cobble runners—Active Movers landscape pad standard villas.

Active Movers garden feature tier

Survey weight photos—rigging quoted; pump drain client or plumber coordinated.

Conclusion

Fountains and courtyard statues move through drain discipline, tier disassembly, rated rigging, and level reinstall—outdoor features survive when water and weight both respected.
author: Active Movers Team
